title: LO2 — Applying Unit 2 concepts to a real dataset: what did you try?

children.body:
The second learning objective asks us to apply the Unit 2 framework to a dataset outside the course examples. I used a small public dataset of student click events from a MOOC. A few things I noticed:

- The preprocessing step took much longer than expected because of missing values in the timestamp column.
- The output matched my intuition for the most part, but one cluster came out looking like an artefact rather than a real pattern.

Has anyone else tried applying this to a real dataset? What did you run into?

I tried it on a small weather dataset. The missing value problem you mention was the same for me — I ended up forward-filling timestamps which felt wrong but got me unstuck. The artefact cluster is interesting: did you check if it corresponded to a specific subset of the data, or was it spread across the full population?

Good instinct to flag the artefact cluster. One way to check: remove it and see if the rest of the structure changes. If it doesn't, it's probably noise. If it does, it may be capturing a real boundary condition the other clusters were absorbing.
